'''Jai Whitbread''' (born 16 January 1998) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} and {{rlp|lf}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Background==
Whitbread was born in [[Tugun, Queensland]], [[Australia]] and raised in [[Tweed Heads, New South Wales]].

He played his junior rugby league for the [[South Tweed RLFC|South Tweed Bears]]. He attended [[Saint Joseph's College, Tweed Heads]] and [[The Southport School]] before being signed by the [[Gold Coast Titans]]. He is the son of former [[Canterbury Bulldogs]] and [[Gold Coast Giants]]/[[Gold Coast Seagulls|Seagulls]] player, [[Greg Whitbread]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Webeck |first=Tony |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/11/14/titans-buy-local-produce-brendan-elliot-jai-whitbread/ |title=Titans buy up big on local produce |publisher=NRL |date=2017-11-14 |access-date=2018-07-15}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2014, while a member of the Gold Coast Titans high performance squad, Whitbread represented the New South Wales Under-16 team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2015/03/28/kanes-special-support-base-/ |title=Kane's special support base |publisher=Titans |date=2015-03-28 |access-date=2018-07-15}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2014/05/20/nsw-under-16---team-announcement/ |title=NSW Under 16 - Team Announcement |publisher=NSWRL |date=2014-05-20 |access-date=2018-07-15}}</ref> In 2016, he joined the [[Brisbane Broncos]], playing for their [[NRL Under-20s|Holden Cup (Under-20s)]] team and representing New South Wales Under-18s team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2016/06/14/nsw-under-18s-team-announcement/ |title=NSW Under-18s Team Announcement |publisher=NSWRL |date=2016-06-14 |access-date=2018-07-15}}</ref>

===2018===
Whitbread began the season playing for the [[Burleigh Bears]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].

In round 18 of the [[2018 NRL season]], Whitbread made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Gold Coast against the [[Sydney Roosters]].  This would be Whitbread's only top grade appearance for the club in 2018.  The Gold Coast would go on to finish in 14th position on the table at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/08/gold-coast-titans-2018-season-review/|title=Gold Coast Titans 2018 season review|website=NRL|date=18 October 2018 }}</ref>

===2019===
Whitbread made a total of 18 appearances for the Gold Coast in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club endured a horror year on and off the field.  During the halfway mark of the season, head coach [[Garth Brennan]] was sacked by the club after a string of poor results.  The Gold Coast managed to win only four games for the entire season and finished last claiming the [[Wooden spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.adelaidenow.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2019-titans-signings-contact-jai-arrow-ash-taylor-mal-meninga-paul-kent/news-story/c3f13d1c4729944c04718c59244b0574/|title=The Titans are full of 'hot air'. Their latest blunder proves it|website=Adelaide Now}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2019/jul/14/gold-coast-titans-sack-coach-garth-brennan/|title=Gold Coast Titans sack coach Garth Brennan|website=The Guardian|date=14 July 2019 }}</ref>

===2020===
Whitbread played nine games for the Gold Coast in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/nrl-2020-nrl-contract-news-signings-transfers-tyrone-roberts-leaves-gold-coast-titans/news-story/fc243b6b9be9fa4e9cf98cd4c2e8d616/|title= Former captain Tyrone Roberts shown the door by Gold Coast Titans|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date= 12 October 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
On 2 August 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=2 Aug 2021|accessdate=3 Aug 2021|title=Sam Stone & Jai Whitbread: Leigh Centurions sign Gold Coast Titans pair|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58063318}}</ref>
On 24 November 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=24 Nov 2021|accessdate=24 Dec 2021|title=Wakefield confirm signing of Australian forward Jai Whitbread|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wakefield-sign-australian-forward-jai-whitbread-leigh/}}</ref>

===2023===
Whitbread played 16 games for Wakefield Trinity in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

On 3 October 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull KR]] following the relegation of [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=3 Oct 2023|accessdate=8 Oct 2023|title=Hull KR win race for Wakefield Trinity forward Jai Whitbread as another exits Super League club|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-kr-win-race-for-wakefield-trinity-forward-jai-whitbread-as-another-exits-super-league-club/}}</ref>

===2024===
Whitbread made his club debut for Hull Kingston Rovers against arch-rivals [[Hull F.C.]] in round 1 of the 2024 Super League season. Hull Kingston Rovers would win the match 22-0.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304214|title=Hull FC 0-22 Hull KR - Rovers win season opener as hosts have two sent off|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 12 October, Whitbread played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===2025===
In round 3 of the 2025 Super League season, Whitbread scored two tries for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 42-0 win over Salford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cd7edwexnd8o|title=Hull KR thrash Salford to continue perfect start|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 7 June, Whitbread played in Hull Kingston Rovers 8-6 Challenge Cup final victory over Warrington. It was the clubs first major trophy in 40 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 18 September, Whitbread played in Hull Kingston Rovers victory over Warrington which saw the club win the League Leaders Shield in the last game of the regular season <ref>https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo</ref>
On 9 October, Whitbread played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Super League Grand Final victory over Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===2026===
On 19 February, Whitbread played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[World Club Challenge]] victory against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/cwy6dlgx3kyt/|title=Relive Hull KR's World Club Challenge win over Brisbane|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
